---
title: "Downloads"
subtitle: "Curated collections and custom downloads"
---

## Curated collections

Pre-packaged collections are available from [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/Big-Life-Lab/cchsflow-docs/releases).

### Core Master Collection (v1.1.0)

Essential English master documentation for CCHS analysis:

- **129 files**: Questionnaires, data dictionaries, user guides, derived variables
- **Years**: 2001-2023 (complete coverage)
- **Language**: English only
- **Document type**: Master files only

[Download from GitHub Releases](https://github.com/Big-Life-Lab/cchsflow-docs/releases/tag/v1.1.0)

## Create a custom collection

To create your own filtered collection:

### Using R

```r
# Clone the repository
# git clone https://github.com/Big-Life-Lab/cchsflow-docs.git

# Load the extraction function
source("R/extract_collection.R")

# Example: French questionnaires only
french_questionnaires <- extract_collection(
 survey = "CCHS",
 collection_name = "cchs-french-questionnaires",
 version = "v1.0.0",
 language = "FR",
 categories = "questionnaire"
)

# Example: Recent years data dictionaries
recent_dd <- extract_collection(
 survey = "CCHS",
 collection_name = "cchs-recent-data-dictionaries",
 version = "v1.0.0",
 years = 2018:2023,
 categories = "data-dictionary"
)
```

### Using the catalog directly

The YAML catalogs contain all file metadata including direct download URLs:

```r
library(yaml)

# Load catalog
catalog <- yaml::read_yaml("data/catalog/cchs_catalog.yaml")

# Filter for specific files
files <- catalog$files
questionnaires_2023 <- files[sapply(files, function(x) {
 x$year == "2023" && x$category == "questionnaire"
})]

# Get download URLs
urls <- sapply(questionnaires_2023, function(x) x$github_url)
```

## Manifests

Each collection includes a CSV manifest with metadata:

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `uid` | Unique identifier |
| `canonical_filename` | Standardised filename |
| `year` | Survey year |
| `category` | Document type |
| `language` | EN or FR |
| `github_url` | Direct download link |

Manifests are stored in [`data/manifests/`](https://github.com/Big-Life-Lab/cchsflow-docs/tree/main/data/manifests).

## AI-assisted exploration

Use the [CCHS Documentation NotebookLM](https://notebooklm.google.com/notebook/d89f1bf8-1eb5-4bc7-bfd4-462be2c01a08) to ask questions about CCHS documentation and get instant answers.
